---
title: "RFCI"
author: "Hansen Zhang"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

library(causalDisco)
library(tidyverse)
data <- read.csv("crashes2023.csv")
```

```{r}
data <- data %>% select(-c(1:4, CrashVehicle, NMCCSimple, LAG_HRS, LAG_MINS, DEATH_TM, VSPD_LIM, DRUGS, TRAV_SP, DISTRACT, DR_DRUGS, EMSAD))
```

```{r}
# 6 categories
data$M_HARM <- dplyr::case_when(
  data$M_HARM %in% c(98, 99)                        ~ NA_character_,
  data$M_HARM == 8                                   ~ "pedestrian",
  data$M_HARM %in% 1:7                               ~ "non_collision",
  data$M_HARM %in% c(12, 14, 45, 51, 54, 55)        ~ "motor_vehicle",
  data$M_HARM %in% c(9, 10, 11, 15, 49, 74)         ~ "other_non_motorist",
  data$M_HARM %in% c(16, 17, 18, 73, 91)            ~ "falling_object",
  data$M_HARM %in% c(19, 20, 21, 23:26, 30:35,
                     38:43, 46, 48, 50, 52, 53,
                     57:59, 72, 93, 44)             ~ "fixed_object",
  TRUE                                               ~ NA_character_
)
```

```{r}
# 3 categories
data$P_CRASH1 <- dplyr::case_when(
  data$P_CRASH1 %in% c(0, 99)                    ~ NA_character_,
  data$P_CRASH1 %in% c(1, 2, 3)                  ~ "straight_travel",
  data$P_CRASH1 %in% c(4, 5, 7, 8, 9)            ~ "stopped_or_parking",
  data$P_CRASH1 %in% c(6, 10, 11, 12, 13, 
                        14, 15, 16, 17, 98)       ~ "complex_maneuver",
  TRUE                                            ~ NA_character_
)
```

```{r}
# 3 categories
data$VE_FORMS <- dplyr::case_when(
  data$VE_FORMS == 1  ~ "single",
  data$VE_FORMS == 2  ~ "two",
  data$VE_FORMS >= 3  ~ "multiple",
  TRUE                ~ NA_character_
)
```

```{r}
# 5 categories
data$ROUTE <- dplyr::case_when(
  data$ROUTE == 99                   ~ NA_character_,
  data$ROUTE %in% c(1, 11)           ~ "interstate",
  data$ROUTE %in% c(2, 3)            ~ "highway",
  data$ROUTE %in% c(4, 5, 6, 12)     ~ "local",          # county/township/municipal
  data$ROUTE %in% c(0, 96)           ~ "unsigned",       # not signed + not in inventory
  data$ROUTE %in% c(10, 13, 95)      ~ "other",
  TRUE                               ~ NA_character_
)
```

```{r}
# 6 categories
data$WEATHER <- dplyr::case_when(
  data$WEATHER %in% c(98, 99)       ~ NA_character_,
  data$WEATHER == 1                  ~ "clear",
  data$WEATHER == 10                 ~ "cloudy",
  data$WEATHER == 2                  ~ "rain",
  data$WEATHER %in% c(3, 4, 11, 12) ~ "frozen_precip",  # snow/sleet/freezing rain
  data$WEATHER == 5                  ~ "fog",
  data$WEATHER %in% c(6, 7, 8)      ~ "other",
  TRUE                               ~ NA_character_
)
```

```{r}
# 3 categories
data$PREV_ACC <- dplyr::case_when(
  data$PREV_ACC %in% c(98, 99, 998) ~ NA_character_,
  data$PREV_ACC == 0                 ~ "none",
  data$PREV_ACC == 1                 ~ "one",
  data$PREV_ACC >= 2                 ~ "multiple",
  TRUE                               ~ NA_character_
)
```

```{r}
# 4 categories
data$VPAVETYP <- dplyr::case_when(
  data$VPAVETYP %in% c(8, 9)      ~ NA_character_,
  data$VPAVETYP %in% c(1, 2, 3)   ~ "paved",        # concrete/asphalt/brick
  data$VPAVETYP %in% c(4, 5)      ~ "unpaved",       # gravel/dirt
  data$VPAVETYP == 0               ~ "non_trafficway",
  data$VPAVETYP == 7               ~ "other",
  TRUE                             ~ NA_character_
)
```

```{r}
# 5 categories
data$VSURCOND <- dplyr::case_when(
  data$VSURCOND %in% c(98, 99)     ~ NA_character_,  # missing
  data$VSURCOND == 1                ~ "dry",
  data$VSURCOND == 2                ~ "wet",
  data$VSURCOND %in% c(3, 4, 10)   ~ "frozen",       # snow/ice/slush
  data$VSURCOND %in% c(0,5,6,7,8,11) ~ "other",        # sand/water/oil/mud etc.
  TRUE                               ~ NA_character_
)
```

```{r}
# 5 categories
data$LGT_COND <- dplyr::case_when(
  data$LGT_COND %in% c(8, 9)  ~ NA_character_,
  data$LGT_COND == 1           ~ "daylight",
  data$LGT_COND == 2           ~ "dark_unlit",
  data$LGT_COND == 3           ~ "dark_lighted",
  data$LGT_COND %in% c(4, 5)  ~ "dawn_dusk",
  data$LGT_COND %in% c(6, 7)  ~ "dark_unknown",  # merge other into dark_unknown
  TRUE                          ~ NA_character_
)
```

```{r}
# ── Convert missingness codes to NA ───────────────────────────────────────────

# AGE; 4 categories
data$AGE[data$AGE %in% c(998, 999)] <- NA

# RUR_URB; 3 categories
data$RUR_URB[data$RUR_URB %in% c(8, 9)] <- NA

# RELJCT1; binary
data$RELJCT1[data$RELJCT1 %in% c(8, 9)] <- NA

# MECISSUE; binary
data$MECISSUE[data$MECISSUE == 2] <- NA

# VIS; binary
data$VIS[data$VIS %in% c(2, 3)] <- NA

# DR_AGE; 4 categories
data$DR_AGE[data$DR_AGE %in% c(998, 999)] <- NA

# DR_SEX; binary
data$DR_SEX[data$DR_SEX %in% c(8, 9)] <- NA

# ── Check missingness rates ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
miss_rates <- sort(colMeans(is.na(data)) * 100, decreasing = TRUE)
round(miss_rates, 1)
```

```{r}
# ── Bin ordinal continuous variables ──────────────────────────────────────────
data$AGE <- cut(data$AGE,
                breaks = c(0, 15, 25, 64, Inf),
                labels = c("child", "young_adult", "adult", "elderly"),
                right  = TRUE)

data$DR_AGE <- cut(data$DR_AGE,
                breaks = c(0, 15, 25, 64, Inf),
                labels = c("child", "young_adult", "adult", "elderly"),
                right  = TRUE)
```

```{r}
data <- as.data.frame(lapply(data, as.factor))
```

```{r}
library(mice)

# Run multiple imputation
# m = number of imputed datasets (5 is standard)
# method = "cart" works well for mixed variable types
imputed <- mice(data,
                m         = 1,
                method    = "cart",
                seed      = 42,
                maxit     = 5,      # proper cycling, just one dataset
                printFlag = TRUE)

# Extract one complete dataset for RFCI
# (for causal discovery we use a single completed dataset)
data_complete <- complete(imputed, action = 1)

# Verify no NAs remain
stopifnot(sum(is.na(data_complete)) == 0)
```
```{r}
write.csv(data_complete, "data_complete.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r}
# ── 1. Build knowledge object with tier structure ─────────────────────────────
know <- knowledge(
  data_complete,
  tier(
    1 ~ AGE + DR_AGE + DR_SEX,
    2 ~ DAY_WEEK + ROUTE + RUR_URB + RELJCT1 +
        LGT_COND + WEATHER + VPAVETYP + VSURCOND + MECISSUE,
    3 ~ DR_DRINK + PREV_ACC + P_CRASH1,
    4 ~ VE_FORMS + M_HARM + VIS,
    5 ~ PED_DEATH
  )
)

# ── 2. Run temporal FCI via disco ─────────────────────────────────────────────

binary_vars <- sapply(data_complete, function(x) nlevels(x) == 2)
data_int <- as.data.frame(lapply(data_complete, as.integer))
my_tfci <- tfci(engine = "causalDisco", test = "reg", alpha = 0.05)

result <- disco(
  data_int,
  my_tfci,
  knowledge = know
)

# ── 3. Plot the PAG ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
plot(result)
```
```{r}
# Flag ALL variables as binary/categorical to avoid spline fitting
binary_vars <- rep(TRUE, ncol(data_int))
names(binary_vars) <- colnames(data_int)

result <- tfci_run(
  data      = data_int,
  knowledge = know,
  alpha     = 0.05,
  test      = reg_test,
  suff_stat = list(data = data_int, binary = binary_vars)
)

plot(result)
```


```{r}
# ── Build suffStat for disCItest ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# dm: integer matrix with levels starting from 1 (pcalg handles this)
# nlev: number of levels per variable
dm   <- data.matrix(data_complete)
nlev <- sapply(data_complete, nlevels)
```

```{r}
# Forbidden edges matrix for pedestrian crash fatality dataset
# Row i -> Col j = TRUE means the directed edge i->j is FORBIDDEN
#
# Causal tier structure:
#   Tier 1 — Fixed pre-existing characteristics (AGE, PREV_ACC, DR_AGE, DR_SEX)
#   Tier 2 — Environmental / infrastructure (DAY_WEEK, ROUTE, RUR_URB, RELJCT1,
#             LGT_COND, WEATHER, VPAVETYP, VSURCOND, MECISSUE)
#   Tier 3 — Pre-crash human state / behavior (DRUGS, TRAV_SP, DR_DRINK,
#             P_CRASH1, DISTRACT, DR_DRUGS)
#   Tier 4 — Crash dynamics (VE_FORMS, M_HARM, VIS)
#   Tier 5 — Post-crash (EMSAD)
#   Tier 6 — Outcome (PED_DEATH)
#
# Forbidden edge rule: tier(i) > tier(j)  =>  i->j is forbidden
# Additionally: PED_DEATH -> anything is forbidden (outcome node)

vars <- c("AGE", "DRUGS", "VE_FORMS", "DAY_WEEK", "ROUTE",
          "RUR_URB", "RELJCT1", "LGT_COND", "WEATHER", "TRAV_SP",
          "DR_DRINK", "PREV_ACC", "VPAVETYP", "VSURCOND", "P_CRASH1",
          "M_HARM", "MECISSUE", "DISTRACT", "VIS", "DR_AGE",
          "DR_SEX", "DR_DRUGS", "PED_DEATH", "EMSAD")

tiers <- c(
  AGE       = 1, DRUGS     = 3, VE_FORMS  = 4, DAY_WEEK  = 2, ROUTE     = 2,
  RUR_URB   = 2, RELJCT1   = 2, LGT_COND  = 2, WEATHER   = 2, TRAV_SP   = 3,
  DR_DRINK  = 3, PREV_ACC  = 1, VPAVETYP  = 2, VSURCOND  = 2, P_CRASH1  = 3,
  M_HARM    = 4, MECISSUE  = 2, DISTRACT  = 3, VIS       = 4, DR_AGE    = 1,
  DR_SEX    = 1, DR_DRUGS  = 3, PED_DEATH = 6, EMSAD     = 5
)

n <- length(vars)
forbidden <- matrix(FALSE, nrow = n, ncol = n, dimnames = list(vars, vars))

for (i in seq_along(vars)) {
  for (j in seq_along(vars)) {
    if (i == j) next
    # Rule 1: higher tier cannot cause lower tier
    if (tiers[vars[i]] > tiers[vars[j]]) {
      forbidden[i, j] <- TRUE
    }
    # Rule 2: outcome cannot cause any predictor
    if (vars[i] == "PED_DEATH") {
      forbidden[i, j] <- TRUE
    }
  }
}

# ── For pcalg ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# pcalg's pc() uses fixedGaps (symmetric, undirected forbidding).
# For *directed* tier constraints, encode the tier ordering as a node-ordering
# via the `skel.method` or orient edges post-hoc using the tier structure.
#
# One approach: after running skeleton(), orient all edges that cross tiers
# using Meek rules or direct orientation. Below we provide the forbidden matrix
# for reference in a custom orientation step.
#
# forbidden[i, j] == TRUE  =>  edge i->j is forbidden
# forbidden is NOT symmetric: forbidden[i,j] may differ from forbidden[j,i]

cat("Forbidden edges built.\n")
cat("Total forbidden directed edges:", sum(forbidden), "\n")
cat("Total possible directed edges: ", n * (n - 1), "\n")
cat("Forbidden fraction: ", round(sum(forbidden) / (n * (n - 1)), 3), "\n")
```
```{r}
forbidden
```

```{r}
# ── Step 1: prefix column names with tier identifiers ─────────────────────────
tier_prefixes <- c(
  AGE = "t1", PREV_ACC = "t1", DR_AGE = "t1", DR_SEX = "t1",
  DAY_WEEK = "t2", ROUTE = "t2", RUR_URB = "t2", RELJCT1 = "t2",
  LGT_COND = "t2", WEATHER = "t2", VPAVETYP = "t2", VSURCOND = "t2", MECISSUE = "t2",
  DRUGS = "t3", TRAV_SP = "t3", DR_DRINK = "t3", P_CRASH1 = "t3",
  DISTRACT = "t3", DR_DRUGS = "t3",
  VE_FORMS = "t4", M_HARM = "t4", VIS = "t4",
  EMSAD = "t5",
  PED_DEATH = "t6"
)

data_tfci <- data
colnames(data_tfci) <- paste0(tier_prefixes[colnames(data)], "_", colnames(data))

# ── Step 2: run TFCI ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# regTest: regression-based independence test, appropriate for categorical data
algo <- tfci(engine = "causalDisco", test = "fisher_z", alpha = 0.05)
tfci_fit <- algo(data_tfci)

# ── Step 3: plot the PAG ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
plot(tfci_fit)

```

```{r}
library(pcalg)
library(dplyr)

# ── 1. Bin ordinal variables ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
data$AGE <- cut(data$AGE,
                breaks = c(0, 15, 25, 64, Inf),
                labels = c("child", "young_adult", "adult", "elderly")) 

data$DR_AGE <- cut(data$DR_AGE,
                breaks = c(0, 15, 25, 64, Inf),
                labels = c("child", "young_adult", "adult", "elderly"),
                right  = TRUE)

data$TRAV_SP <- cut(data$TRAV_SP,
                    breaks = c(0, 25, 45, 65, Inf),
                    labels = c("residential", "urban", "highway", "freeway"))

data$EMSAD <- case_when(
  data$EMSAD == 0  ~ "died_before_ems",
  data$EMSAD == -1 ~ NA_character_,
  data$EMSAD <= 15 ~ "fast",
  data$EMSAD <= 30 ~ "moderate",
  data$EMSAD > 30  ~ "slow",
  TRUE             ~ NA_character_
)

# ── 2. Convert all to factors ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
data <- as.data.frame(lapply(data, as.factor)) %>% 
  drop_na

# Verify no variable has 0 levels
stopifnot(all(sapply(data, nlevels) > 0))

# ── 3. Build suffStat ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
dm       <- data.matrix(data) - 1L # integer matrix, levels 1..k
nlev     <- sapply(data, nlevels)
suffStat <- list(dm = dm, nlev = nlev, adaptDF = FALSE)
```

```{r}
# ── 4. Run RFCI ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# rfci has no forbEdges parameter — temporal constraints applied post-hoc
set.seed(42)
rfci_fit <- rfci(
  suffStat  = suffStat,
  indepTest = disCItest,
  labels    = colnames(data),
  NAdelete  = FALSE,
  alpha     = 0.05,
  verbose = FALSE,
  m.max = 3
)

# ── 5. Post-hoc: enforce tier ordering in the PAG ────────────────────────────
# PAG adjacency matrix encoding: amat[i,j] = mark at vertex j on edge i-j
#   0 = no edge
#   1 = circle (o)
#   2 = arrowhead (>)  at j
#   3 = tail    (-)  at j
# For i -> j: amat[i,j] = 2 (arrowhead at j), amat[j,i] = 3 (tail at i)

tiers <- c(
  AGE=1, DRUGS=3, VE_FORMS=4, DAY_WEEK=2, ROUTE=2,
  RUR_URB=2, RELJCT1=2, LGT_COND=2, WEATHER=2, TRAV_SP=3,
  DR_DRINK=3, PREV_ACC=1, VPAVETYP=2, VSURCOND=2, P_CRASH1=3,
  M_HARM=4, MECISSUE=2, DISTRACT=3, VIS=4, DR_AGE=1,
  DR_SEX=1, DR_DRUGS=3, PED_DEATH=6, EMSAD=5
)

vars <- colnames(data)
amat <- rfci_fit@amat

for (i in seq_along(vars)) {
  for (j in seq_along(vars)) {
    if (i == j) next
    # Edge exists and lower tier -> higher tier: enforce i -> j
    if (amat[i, j] != 0 && tiers[vars[i]] < tiers[vars[j]]) {
      amat[i, j] <- 2   # arrowhead at j
      amat[j, i] <- 3   # tail at i
    }
  }
}

# ── 6. Plot ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
library(Rgraphviz)
plot(rfci_fit)   # original PAG before tier enforcement
```


```{r}
data_int <- as.data.frame(lapply(data_complete, as.integer))

reg_test(
  x = 1,
  y = 2,
  conditioning_set = NULL,
  suff_stat = list(data = data_int, binary = binary_vars)
)
```
